element_introduction_order.mastery_context: foundational movement across linear early stages
primary weapon for engaging enemies in platforming sequences
ranged attack option, requires charging for power shots
vertical traversal mechanic introduced alongside basic platforming
executed by pressing down and jump simultaneously, enables secret passages
alternative ranged weapon with distinct trajectory
advanced offensive tool for defeating tougher enemies and environmental obstacles
shift from linear progression to nonlinear routing and spatial navigation
mandatory confrontations blocking stage progression, require mastery of combat tools
optional secrets encouraging exploration and replay
death mechanic that reduces penalty, tied to nearest checkpoint

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: early stages emphasize linear action-platforming with consistent obstacle flow; mid-to-late stages shift to exploratory maze navigation with higher cognitive and mechanical demands

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: crouching beneath obstacles to access enclosed side-passage areas containing secrets
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: hidden letters spelling PITFALL distributed throughout stages; beneficial items and ammunition caches placed off main path

difficulty_curve.early_game: linear stages with straightforward left-to-right progression, basic enemy types, whip and sling stone introduction, manageable obstacle placement
difficulty_curve.mid_game: introduction of additional projectile weapons (boomerangs, explosives), more complex enemy behavior, shift toward maze-like layouts requiring navigation choices
difficulty_curve.late_game: maze-heavy exploration with multiple route options, cumulative enemy and hazard density, boss encounters with mechanical complexity, resource management emphasis
notable_spikes: first boss encounter (establishes combat gate mechanic)
transition from linear to maze-like stage design (cognitive shift)
late-stage boss confrontations requiring mastered weapon mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Horizontally-driven progression with distributed obstacles, enemies, and platforming challenges. Player moves left-to-right through clear stage architecture with minimal alternative routing.
Stage design becomes nonlinear, requiring player to navigate multiple routes and make directional choices to reach objectives. Emphasizes spatial navigation over reflexive action.
Mandatory combat confrontations blocking stage progression. Occur at intervals throughout game to create clear pacing breakpoints and mechanical mastery gates.

sources_used: wikipedia (Pitfall: The Mayan Adventure) - gameplay description, mechanics, stage structure
tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - development notes on visual style transition
